records.ety: [OF. impost, F. impot, LL. impostus, fr. L. impostus, p. p. of imponere to impose. See Impone.]

defs: That which is imposed or levied; a tax, tribute, or duty; especially, a duty or tax laid by government on goods imported into a country.
quotes: Even the ship money . . . Johnson could not pronounce to have been an unconstitutional impost.

defs: The top member of a pillar, pier, wall, etc., upon which the weight of an arch rests.
notes: ☞ The impost is called continuous, if the moldings of the arch or architrave run down the jamb or pier without a break.
